filename: 17.5.24 Meeting with Anniina on image processing.pptx
relative_path: Data & Figures/Data/1. t-CycIF/4_4 - PRITTI cohort experiment/4_4 analysis files/2.Clustering_attempts/17.5.24 Meeting with Anniina on image processing.pptx
full_text:
Cell cycle project meeting

Ada-Anniina 16.5.23

pChk1, PD1 and TCF did not work
Cyclin A was finally replaced by Geminin

TIGIT was not working in the validations and therefore it was not added

Some samples were very detached in cycle 12 and cycle 13 was added as an accessory cycle, which might be used for other projects/hypothesis drafting

Spacers caused focus issues, and they were added in cycle 8 only for the small samples, after all cell cycle nuclear stainings were ready

The final panel:

28 samples:
5 Bad quality
23 Medium/High

Progress so far:

Stitched and segmented, with some shift errors
Cylinter: cropping out bad-quality areas and poor cycle correlation-areas 
This needs to be revisited, an enormous amount of DC and CD4/8 areas have been dropped
Cell type calling:
Basic phenotypes (Tumor, Stromal, DC, M1, M2, CD8 T cells, CD4 T cells)
Attempted TRIBUS, only worked in 50% of the samples
The other 50% have unusually high levels of CD8 in the tumor and I have preferred to gate these
NK cells, B cells and others will be explored in the future

Expected marker oscillation throughout the cell cycle

How to closely study the cell cycle phases?
Are my markers really working?

Brief exploratory analysis made sense

3D representation effectively recapitulates cell cycle

G0/G1

S

M

S107_iOme tumor cells

DDR markers across cell cycle phases differ within patients

Tumor S107

Tumor S268

Tumor S057

Distinct proliferation dynamics 
could be explored across phenotypes

Tumor

Stroma

Are these arrested?

Multimarker proliferation scores 
might not be that reliable to classify cells

Challenges

Operational 
Very large dataset
Complex staining patterns, tough visual exploration of the results
Technical 
Correct normalization-scaling
Learning to perform pseudotime analysis
Scientific
Narrowing-down the questions 
Integrating findings from very distinct platforms

Scientific questions

How can we better classify these “arrested” phenotypes?
The preliminary analysis shows how the number of cells in these phases varies across samples, I would like to subcategorize patients based on those (eg S-phase enriched, M-enriched). CCNE1 rich-patterned patients seemed to have increased S-phase cells, which correlates with their biology. Maybe this strategy could also help stratifying the patients.
This data might be useful to create a replication stress score, and to also address checkpoint recovery

Objectives mid-term

End cell type calling - focusing on narrow cell-type set
Modelling cell cycle stages in t-CycIF
Multidimensionality reduction
Downsampling methods
Clustering methods
Pseudotime analysis?
Exploring:
Role of DNA-damage in the cell cycle phases
Correlations with clinical data
Spatial dimension of the results
Selecting neighborhoods for spatial transcriptomics
Functional tests and validations? -> TBD with María

What to bring to the EACR?

End cell type calling - focusing on narrow cell-type set
Modelling cell cycle stages in t-CycIF
Multidimensionality reduction
Clustering methods
Exploring:
Role of DNA-damage in the cell cycle phases
Correlations with clinical data
